title: Alien vs Predator vs Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: Alien vs. Predator vs. Evangelion             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sun Dec 30, 2007 2:07 pm Okay, so after a bit of discussion with Anti-Goth in the IRC, I've decided to bring up an important question for those who know Evangelion best. Hopefully, the Eva experts on this forum will be able to shed some light on the question we've been talking about. Based on visual evidence from the series, as well as supporting evidence from the Red Cross Book and CI, we can be certain of two things: 1.) Humans come from Lilith. 2.) Angels come from Adam. So, that brings to light to question that seems to have caused a great deal of confusion. Namely... 3.) Where do Aliens and Predators come from?

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Sun Dec 30, 2007 2:22 pm Is it really relevent, or are you taking it seriously? If you are taking it seriously, it is possible from either one of them. Just the evolution process took a radical turn within the billions of years.

[]:             Quote Post by yokuchi » Sun Dec 30, 2007 2:26 pm The Imperialist wrote: it is possible from either one of them. Just the evolution process took a radical turn within the billions of years.

[]:             Quote Post by Anti-Goth » Sun Dec 30, 2007 2:29 pm The Imperialist wrote: If you are taking it seriously, it is possible from either one of them. Just the evolution process took a radical turn within the billions of years.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Sun Dec 30, 2007 2:34 pm Ummm... exactly. Maybe I should have made that more clearer, but yes, this 'evolution' took place on other planets, BUT this is assuming that Aliens and Predators are in the same 'universe' as Eva.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Dec 30, 2007 2:41 pm The Imperialist wrote: Ummm... exactly. Maybe I should have made that more clearer, but yes, this 'evolution' took place on other planets, BUT this is assuming that Aliens and Predators are in the same 'universe' as Eva.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Sun Dec 30, 2007 2:44 pm Who said the FAR are extinct? But seriously, does this actually really matter? (serious questions)

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Sun Dec 30, 2007 2:45 pm well, there ya go again with yer fanwanking :roll:

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Sun Dec 30, 2007 2:48 pm What? What did I do?

[]:             Quote Post by Anti-Goth » Sun Dec 30, 2007 3:00 pm Reichu wrote: Were the FAR wiped out by xenomorphs? Is there a white giant slumbering on the Predators' homeworld?

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Sun Dec 30, 2007 3:04 pm May somebody forgive me. *Puts neck through a noose* *Kicks chair away* *ZZZAAAAPPP (divine intervention)* THUD! Alright, somebody did forgive me AACCKK *cardiac arrest*

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Sun Dec 30, 2007 3:08 pm well, this must be the first recorded suicide on EGF

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Sun Dec 30, 2007 3:09 pm I hope you did not take it seriously.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Dec 30, 2007 3:15 pm The xenomorphs were created for military purposes by the Space Jockeys. At least, that's what some of the extended material states. In any case, there were more Seeds of Life than just Adam and Lilith. Seeds were spread to various planets. Therefore, the Space Jockeys and the Yautja came from non-Adam, non-Lilith Seeds of Life (though there may just be Adam-type and Lilith-type; if that's the case, they probably both came from Lilith-type or a facehugging might result in Third Impact).

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Sun Dec 30, 2007 3:24 pm DOn't feel bad, but my hand is temptingly reaching for the window lock.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Dec 30, 2007 3:30 pm Imp, one complaint about how you think the thread sucks will suffice, okay?

[]:             Quote Post by Anti-Goth » Sun Dec 30, 2007 3:35 pm NAveryW wrote: there were more Seeds of Life than just Adam and Lilith. Seeds were spread to various planets. Therefore, the Space Jockeys and the Yautja came from non-Adam, non-Lilith Seeds of Life (though there may just be Adam-type and Lilith-type; if that's the case, they probably both came from Lilith-type or a facehugging might result in Third Impact).

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Dec 30, 2007 3:40 pm But then wouldn't the Space Jockeys and the Yautja technically need to be humans as all Angels (= Definition 2) are, or are we going to derail certain "assumptions" here?

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Dec 30, 2007 3:46 pm Well, all right, then. The Space Jockeys and Yautja could be human in the same sense that the Adam-derived Angels are human. We may need a fourth Judeo-Christian progenitor, though. Does one exist? Or are we going to have to go to other religions? OOH! OOH! WAIT!!! Scratch that. I've got a great idea, and I'm going to use it for a doujinshi. That's right, I'm not going to write a regular fanfiction. It's cool enough to be a comic. /No, it will not have pr0n. Thanks, Brik and Reichu!!! EDIT: I have dibs on the first EvangAlien fan work. It probably won't involve the Predator, though; someone else may use him if desired.

[]:             Quote Post by Skullraper » Sun Dec 30, 2007 3:50 pm Anti-Goth wrote: I can feel an interesting crossover comin' on! =D
